Evertune SAP Analysis

This page contains SAP-specific GEO analysis and is restricted to SAP employees.

🔒 Enter the access code shared with SAP PMM team
⭐ Vendor Analysis

Evertune SAP HCM GEO Analysis

Category: Human Capital Management Software · March 2026

📊 Executive Summary

Evertune analyzed SAP's visibility across 11 major AI platforms in the Human Capital Management category. Here's what the models say about SAP — and how you compare to competitors.

68
Average AI Brand Score
+3
Sentiment Score (Neutral)
89
Best: Perplexity
42
Worst: Meta AI

💡 Key Takeaway

SAP has strong visibility on ChatGPT (82), Gemini (81), and Perplexity (89) — but underperforms on Google's search products (Gemini Search: 44) and Meta AI (42). The models describe SAP positively for functionality and compliance, but consistently flag "steep learning curve" and "high cost" as concerns.

💬 How Do the Models Describe SAP?

Positive Associations

  • Comprehensive Suite — breadth of functionality
  • Global Compliance — regulatory coverage
  • Advanced Analytics — data-driven decisions
  • Single Source of Truth — unified platform
  • Deep Integration — enterprise connectivity

Negative Associations (Opportunity Areas)

  • Steep Learning Curve — usability concern
  • High Total Cost — pricing perception
  • Less Intuitive — UX friction
  • Complex Implementation — time-to-value
  • Significant Investment — budget concerns

🏆 How Do the Models Describe Competitors?

Workday

+28 Positive

Gold Standard, Real-Time Analytics, User-Friendly Interface

Ceridian Dayforce

+23 Positive

Real-Time Payroll, Unified Platform, Robust Engine

UKG Pro

+7 Neutral

Robust Reporting, Advanced Analytics, Comprehensive

SAP

+3 Neutral

Comprehensive, Global Compliance, High Cost

Oracle Cloud

-1 Negative

Complex Implementation, Less Intuitive, Significant Investment

⚠️ Competitive Gap

Workday has the strongest sentiment (+28) and is frequently described as the "gold standard" with a user-friendly interface. SAP's neutral sentiment (+3) reflects a perception problem: the models see SAP as powerful but difficult. Oracle is the only competitor with negative sentiment.

📈 How Often Do the Models Recommend SAP?

The AI Brand Score measures the probability of a model recommending SAP on an unaided basis — i.e., when SAP is not mentioned in the consumer's prompt.

Platform Score Assessment
Perplexity 89 🟢 Excellent
ChatGPT 82 🟢 Strong
Gemini 81 🟢 Strong
ChatGPT Search 80 🟢 Strong
Average 68 —
Google AI Mode 63 🟡 Moderate
DeepSeek 63 🟡 Moderate
Google AI Overview 58 🟡 Moderate
Claude 57 🟡 Moderate
Gemini Search 44 🔴 Weak
Meta AI 42 🔴 Weak

🎯 Priority Platforms

  • Gemini Search (44) — Google's search-enhanced model underperforms vs. base Gemini (81). Likely a content indexing issue.
  • Meta AI (42) — Heavy reliance on Instagram/Facebook signals. Social proof gap.
  • Claude (57) — Anthropic's model is increasingly influential in enterprise. Worth monitoring.

🏁 How Often Do Models Recommend Competitors?

SAP ranks #2 in overall AI visibility behind Workday, but leads on specific platforms (Perplexity, ChatGPT). The long tail of HCM vendors (Gusto, Rippling, Paylocity) have significantly lower visibility.

💡 Market Position

Workday dominates on average visibility, but SAP's strength on Perplexity (89) and ChatGPT (82) is a competitive advantage for enterprise buyers who use those platforms. Focus on defending these positions while improving Google and Meta platforms.

🎯 Buyer Preference Analysis

How likely are the models to recommend SAP for specific buyer preferences?

SAP Strengths (High Recommendation Rate)

  • Scalability — Models consistently recommend SAP for large enterprises
  • Security — Strong association with enterprise-grade security
  • Features — Comprehensive functionality recognized
  • Customization — Flexibility for complex requirements

SAP Weaknesses (Low Recommendation Rate)

  • Price — Rarely recommended when cost is a priority
  • Usability — Models hesitate to recommend for ease-of-use
  • User Experience — UX perception drags down recommendations

📊 Competitive Positioning Radar

This radar chart shows how SAP compares to Oracle Cloud, UKG Pro, Ceridian Dayforce, and Workday across key buyer preference dimensions.

🔍 Reading the Chart

Workday (red) leads on Usability and User Experience. SAP (dark blue) leads on Security and Features. The opportunity: close the UX gap while defending functional leadership.

📝 Content Strategy Recommendations

Evertune's AI Education Briefs connect SAP's strengths with the words that resonate most with the models. Priority topic: Price — reframe the cost conversation.

Recommended Messaging: "Investment in Innovation"

The pricing structure of SAP signifies a commitment to quality and innovation, ensuring that clients receive not just software, but a powerful platform that continuously evolves with technology and best practices in Human Capital Management.

Suggested Keywords

  • Investment in Innovation
  • Best Practices
  • Commitment to Quality
  • Long-Term Value
  • Scalable Solutions
  • Continuous Evolution
  • Powerful Platform
  • Business Transformation

✍️ Content Action Items

  • Create thought leadership content reframing "cost" as "investment in innovation"
  • Publish ROI case studies emphasizing long-term value over upfront cost
  • Develop comparison content that contextualizes TCO vs. competitors
  • Target publications that influence model training (see next section)

🌐 Influential Domains

Which domains are having the largest influence on how AI models perceive the HCM category?

Top Influential Domains

Domain Type Source Share
paylocity.com Corporate 6.06%
g2.com Earned 2.80%
peoplemanagingpeople.com Earned 2.44%
whatfix.com Corporate 1.98%
rippling.com Corporate 1.79%
linkedin.com Social 1.65%
gartner.com Earned 1.53%

📢 Distribution Priorities

  • G2 — Ensure SAP reviews are current and positive
  • peoplemanagingpeople.com — Pitch thought leadership content
  • LinkedIn — Increase HCM-related content publishing
  • Gartner — Leverage analyst relationships for citation

🔗 URL-Level Opportunities

2,323
Total URLs Analyzed
0.1%
Owned URL Share
9.0%
Strength URL Share
11.4%
Opportunity URL Share

⚠️ Critical Finding

Only 0.1% of influential URLs are SAP-owned. The models are learning about HCM from third-party sources, not from SAP directly. Priority: increase SAP's content footprint on URLs the models are actually reading.

🤖 Publisher Partnerships for Model Training

Which publishers should SAP partner with to educate the foundational models? The foundational models form a model's deep knowledge and are the source of truth for AI agents.

Top AI Education Score Publishers

Domain Type Avg Score OpenAI Google Claude
whatfix.com Corporate 9.7 9.9 9.5 9.7
softwareadvice.com Earned 9.5 9.6 9.6 9.5
technologyadvice.com Affiliate 9.4 9.8 8.6 9.4
rippling.com Corporate 9.4 9.7 9.1 9.4
g2.com Earned 9.3 10.0 9.2 9.3

🤝 Partnership Recommendations

  • G2 (10.0 OpenAI score) — Highest influence on ChatGPT. Prioritize review management.
  • Software Advice / Technology Advice — Pitch SAP content for inclusion
  • Whatfix — Explore co-marketing opportunities
  • Note: Perplexity blocks all crawlers — requires direct data partnerships

🚀 Recommended Next Steps

  1. Address the "cost" narrative — Create content repositioning SAP pricing as "investment in innovation"
  2. Fix Google visibility gap — Investigate why Gemini Search (44) underperforms vs. Gemini (81)
  3. Increase owned URL influence — Only 0.1% of influential URLs are SAP-owned. Publish more content on sap.com
  4. Target G2 and Software Advice — These sites have highest model influence. Ensure SAP presence is strong
  5. Monitor Meta AI — Lowest score (42). Consider social content strategy to improve Instagram/Facebook signals
